name: Doctor Authority
description: Authority figure (doctor / dermatologist / nutritionist / dentist / pharmacist) in a white coat or scrubs explains why a product, ingredient, or approach works. The dominant nutra / supplement / skincare / wellness DTC ad pattern: white coat = trust shortcut, mechanism explanation = 'I understand why this works' feeling. 20-45s, 9:16. Format: 0-3s authority hook ('As a dermatologist, I tell every patient…') → 3-25s mechanism explanation (what the ingredient does, the science) → 25-40s product / ingredient name + recommendation → 40-45s soft CTA. Walks a regulatory line — refusing to impersonate real-named medical professionals without consent (AGENTS.md hard rule #3); platform-side C2PA provenance metadata is emitted by render (genai:true) without a visible on-screen overlay.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter for the authority-figure keyframe (white coat or scrubs, clinical setting, professional posture, calm eye contact). One canonical hero shot drives identity for every clip.
stackSummary.i2v: google/veo-3.1 via OpenRouter for the talking-head clip (premium model — trust signal depends on micro-expression realism, lip-sync, calm eye contact). Optional kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro for short ingredient / mechanism / product cutaway b-roll (3-5s clips). generate_audio: false for both — VO is ElevenLabs.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2, stability 0.45 (calm authoritative — NOT excited, NOT salesy), similarity 0.75, ~130 WPM. Voice cast: clinical-warm, mid-register, no upsell hype.
stackSummary.captions: ElevenLabs Scribe for word-level timestamps. MinimalCaptions (bottom-third, white-on-soft-shadow) or restrained Hormozi (no screaming yellow — too tabloid for medical authority).
stackSummary.music: Optional. Off by default. If used: ElevenLabs Music ambient bed at -25dB (barely-there tonal pad). No drums, no build, no drop — anything energetic breaks the authority signal.

constants.structure: 0-3s authority hook → 3-25s mechanism explainer → 25-40s ingredient/product mention → 40-45s soft CTA

variationAxes: profession (dermatologist, nutritionist, dentist, pharmacist, cardiologist, GP)
setting (clinical office, home office with credentials wall, pharmacy counter, neutral studio)
real-vs-AI (real licensed actor with consent / AI-avatar)
VO language (any — emotional arc reads through tone and white-coat visual)
doNotCopyLiterally: Authority script, ingredient name, profession choice, exact prompts. Vibe / format reference only.
referenceNotes: Two gates apply. (1) Branded product gate: if a specific branded supplement / skincare / nutra product is recommended on-screen, a product reference (photo / packaging / logo) MUST exist at workspace/projects/<id>/assets/uploaded/ — else refuse (AGENTS.md hard rule #3). AI-improvised branding on a real product reads as fake and is a regulatory landmine. (2) Real-named professional gate: if the brief invokes a real-named medical professional (Dr. Jane Doe, Dermatologist at X clinic), refuse outright unless the user provides written consent — impersonating a real-named medical professional is a hard rule #3 violation and a defamation risk. Generic 'a dermatologist' / 'a nutritionist' is fine with an AI-generated synthetic figure; platform-side C2PA provenance metadata is emitted by render (genai:true) — no visible on-screen overlay (retention drops with visible AI labels). Therapeutic / curative claims ('cures acne', 'reverses diabetes') are refused regardless — they require regulatory backing this template does not produce.
